posts.raw_content:
So I've had a lot of experience with speaking to call centers and outsourced personnel since Trump came into office. Before he came into office? Almost all Indian, 24/7. After? We're seeing things like more American workers during the day and foreign workers during the night, but they're not Indian anymore, they're in places like the Philippines. Probably better for security. 

So it seems India has started to pay a price for all its scam antics, and was also probably found by many companies to be the source of a lot of fraud. Indian call centers may have been limited to what data they could access, but they could probably do something with it, especially when you consider that information like name and last 4 numbers of social or stuff like that was what companies might rely on, and so giving foreign call centers access to that, while also being reliant on that type of authentication, meant that the bar was lowered enough for them to be able to access things with what limited access they were given in the first place.

It's almost a silent change that most people wouldn't immediately notice. But it seems that things like what foreign personnel and where has been shifted since Trump came into office.
posts.author: preferredfault
posts.title: Something interesting I noticed since Trump came into office...
posts.content:
So I've had a lot of experience with speaking to call centers and outsourced personnel since Trump came into office. Before he came into office? Almost all Indian, 24/7. After? We're seeing things like more American workers during the day and foreign workers during the night, but they're not Indian anymore, they're in places like the Philippines. Probably better for security.

So it seems India has started to pay a price for all its scam antics, and was also probably found by many companies to be the source of a lot of fraud. Indian call centers may have been limited to what data they could access, but they could probably do something with it, especially when you consider that information like name and last 4 numbers of social or stuff like that was what companies might rely on, and so giving foreign call centers access to that, while also being reliant on that type of authentication, meant that the bar was lowered enough for them to be able to access things with what limited access they were given in the first place.

It's almost a silent change that most people wouldn't immediately notice. But it seems that things like what foreign personnel and where has been shifted since Trump came into office.
